programmes.show_description: Brand new series: Can Margot's 700-piece Barbie collection keep her home on the water afloat? Ben's hoping to turn a vintage audio kit into his lifelong dream of a Spitfire flight. (Ep5/6)
Staff nurse Sophie has only been at King's for three weeks. One of her patients is James, a chef who accidentally stabbed himself in the thigh while chopping tomatoes.
A look at the bond between parents and children, including the youngest patient yet to be featured on the series, and what happens when the roles are reversed due to illness.
A selection of favourite investigations. Matt Tebbutt investigates oysters in China. Jimmy Doherty checks out London dry gin. Kate Quilton asks if herbal bedtime teas can help you nod off.
Discover the very latest must-haves for your home and garden with Must Have Ideas.
Discover the very latest must-haves for your home and garden with Must Have Ideas.
The campers end their tour of north Wales at The Oyster Farm Caravan Site on Anglesey with oyster gin and an aquatic tour of Puffin Island, before they pick their perfect pitch.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: Henry Cole monkeys around with a monkey bike, while Simon O'Brien uses all his skills to restore an old racing bike.
Debbie and Guy search for their perfect forever home where they can start up their own gîte business in the Charente region of France. Jasmine Harman shows them what their £135k can buy.
North East. Clothes shop owner Lee hopes to impress her guests with an evening dedicated to her favourite food. But will her not-so-Greek salad and make-your-own wine be a hit? (Ep1/5)
North East. Hairdresser Simon hopes his tried-and-tested menu can bag him the grand. But will a disappointing dessert and some dodgy dinner table discussions ruin his chances? (Ep2/5)
North East. Nurse Sophie hopes to unleash her guests' inner children with her kids' party theme. There's a sauce squabble, and one guest uses a spoon to eat their burger! (Ep3/5)
North East. Sexual health nurse Jade throws a Wild West-themed party in a bid to rope herself the money. But can she serve a remotely edible dessert? (Ep4/5)
North East. Emergency planner James serves up a carefully constructed menu. But as his guests reveal their true feelings, will his effort-to-reward ratio pay off? (Ep5/5)
Mum and daughter Ann and Rebecca kick off the competition at Grange Farm Park in Lincolnshire. But tensions bubble when one pair of guests discover they're the only ones without a hot tub.
Marilyn and Neil's Edgecliff Boutique B&B in Kent boasts breathtaking views. But will a slow breakfast leave their chances of winning balancing on a knife edge?
The B&Bers visit Vera's Kitchen and B&B in Gloucestershire, where Warren's forced to host solo. Will a game of bar billiards help to win the week, or will breakfast lower egg-spectations?
Father and son Bis and Deep are the final hosts, at The Preston Hotel in Yeovil. Their USP is value for money, but some guests think the furniture is more cheap than cheerful...
On an emotional payment day, the B&Bers air their grievances on everything from slow breakfasts to dusty chandeliers. Who will come out on top and claim the crown?
Citroën SM: Tim Shaw and Fuzz Townshend fix up a 1973 Citroën SM, one of the greatest ever French cars. But this one has suffered a catastrophic engine failure.
TVR Grantura: In Norfolk, Tim Shaw and Fuzz Townshend fix up a 1962 TVR Grantura, a once classy British sports car that has fallen into a terrible state of disrepair.
Pomeranian Prince is ready to rule, but will the Acton family be his subjects? Winnie's constant chewing of squeaky toys could be his downfall with the Johnsons.
In 2011, Edward and Hazel set out to build a lighthouse on the north Devon coast. By 2019, they were near bankruptcy with just a rusting shell. Three years on, has anything has changed?
Brand new series - Four Funerals and a Wedding: An actor is shot on a film set during an exchange of supposedly blank gunfire. Was it an accident? In French/subs.
Two-year-old Max has come to A&E struggling to breathe. Maureen has fallen down the stairs and broken her ankle. And motorcyclist Josh has been in a high-speed accident.
Eighteen-year-old Kye is rushed to Queen's after going into cardiac arrest during a football training session. Student Phoebe has hurt her nose after tripping over in a library.
Paramedics treat an unconscious man who's taken drugs, a young person in police custody who has a high heart rate, and a teenager who's having a seizure.
Two-year-old Max has come to A&E struggling to breathe. Maureen has fallen down the stairs and broken her ankle. And motorcyclist Josh has been in a high-speed accident.
Eighteen-year-old Kye is rushed to Queen's after going into cardiac arrest during a football training session. Student Phoebe has hurt her nose after tripping over in a library.
Kate, Jimmy and Matt revisit some of their favourite investigations. What's the difference between white and dark rum? What's the jelly in a jelly bean? And why do anchovies taste salty?
Discover the very latest must-haves for your home and garden with Must Have Ideas.
Discover the very latest must-haves for your home and garden with Must Have Ideas.
Nicole and Lee are moving permanently to Aude in the south of France and need a place with plenty of space. Can Jean Johansson find the perfect fit for their budget of £270k?
Jasmine Harman jets off to the east coast of Cyprus to help former Royal Navy engineer David and his partner Fiona find the perfect berth from five amazing options.
Greg and Tina want a holiday home in western Cyprus to share with all their family. Leah Charles-King shows them the best of what their £190k can buy; does that include their perfect place?
Jill hopes to charm her rivals with her authentic 17th-century B&B, The Castle Arms Inn, in Snape, North Yorkshire. But will a bad night's sleep for two guests scupper her chances?
The B&Bers head off to the great outdoors at Eden Valley Glamping site in Armathwaite, Cumbria, where hosts Liz and Dave hope to convert their guests with their back-to-basics approach.
Neil and Mary Blackburn welcome their rivals to Fernie Castle in Fife. There's plenty of traditional Scots splendour on show, but two guests aren't impressed by the continental breakfast.
The last visit of the week is to the Cadillac Kustomz Hotel on the Isle of Bute, where owners Brody and Rima hope to rock their guests' worlds with a music-filled retro retreat.
On payment day, there are curtain complaints and breakfast blues as the B&Bers iron out their issues, before they find out how much they've been paid and who's won the week.
Durham. Receptionist Linzey plans to woo her guests with some flirtatious frolics and a menu of food from the heart. Can she seduce her guests or will it be a date-night disaster? (Ep1/5)
Durham. Rob aims to teach his guests a lesson in hosting with an evening based around school, from swimming to home economics. And a new guest joins the group in place of another. (Ep2/5)
Durham. Event planner Kate throws a Roaring 20s-themed party with a vegan menu. But will the meat eaters be left wanting, and can a high-energy Charleston lesson get everyone going? (Ep3/5)
Durham. Ex-trampolinist Matty hosts an Ibiza-themed party, complete with Spanish food and full-on clubbing. But will his fiesta and impromptu flips get his guests feeling the vibe? (Ep4/5)
Durham. Health worker and party girl Kenya hosts a safari-themed evening featuring East African food. But will her wild night have her guests leaping for joy or running for cover? (Ep5/5)
The competition kicks off at the Star & Garter in Linlithgow, West Lothian, with host Denise and her daughter Carina. But cleanliness and connectivity concern the guests at feedback time.
Shona and John Mitchell host at Larch Green Lodges in Langbank. Their guests are delighted by the sweeping views, but their hosting is blemished by a breakfast black pudding blunder.
The B&Bers head for the Distant Hills Guest House in Spean Bridge, run by Dani and Cameron. The guests are blown away by the scenic views, but a dirty discovery leads to tears at feedback.
The final visit takes the B&Bers to 18 Craigmillar Park in Edinburgh. The guests are impressed by host Craig's military precision, but their feedback leaves him in quite a huff!
It's payment day and the B&Bers meet to discuss dirty remarks and filthy findings. After their differences are aired and discrepancies debated, the winner is announced.
This episode explores the power of love when a man is involved in a serious bicycle collision and an elderly lady becomes unwell at home.
Staff nurse Sophie has only been at King's for three weeks. One of her patients is James, a chef who accidentally stabbed himself in the thigh while chopping tomatoes.
A look at the bond between parents and children, including the youngest patient yet to be featured on the series, and what happens when the roles are reversed due to illness.
Paramedics treat two children who've been hit by a car, a man who's having back-to-back epileptic seizures and a four-month-old baby who's struggling to breathe.
